heading: 2023 Releases
body:
- Supports auto-index creation for Atlas Serverless instances.

- Supports migrations between sharded clusters with a different
  number of shards for MongoDB 6.0+ with Atlas Live Migration. To learn
  more, see [Live Migrate a MongoDB 6.0.5 or Later Cluster into Atlas](c2c-pull-live-migration).

- Integrates push-based logging with AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service). To learn more, see [Push Logs to AWS S3 bucket](mongodb-logs-push).

- Supports programmatic IP access list for secure Atlas communication
  by using customer-managed encryption keys. To learn more, see [Enable Customer Key Management for Search Nodes](atlas-add-nodes-to-encrypted-cluster).

- Rejects [Test Failover](test-failover) if cluster is not fully healthy.

- Adds the [returnAllControlPlaneIpAddresses API endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listcontrolplaneipaddresses)
  that returns a list of inbound and outbound Atlas control plane IP
  addresses in CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) categorized by cloud provider and region. To learn more,
  see [Allow Access From the Atlas Control Plane](atlas-ear-allow-access-via-control-plane).

* Increases the minimum threshold for archiving data after 7 days from 100 kB to 5 MiB.
  To learn more, see [Limitations](config-online-archive-limitations).

- Makes MongoDB 7.1 generally available for all deployments.

* Provides preview of dark mode for the Atlas UI. To learn more,
  see [the MongoDB blog](https://www.mongodb.com/blog/post/dark-mode-for-atlas-now-available-public-preview).

* Supports node availability zones for replica set tags. To learn more,
  see [pre-defined replica set tags](replica-set-tags).

- Adds new regions for Azure (Microsoft Azure) NVMe (non-volatile memory express) clusters.

* Supports Push Live migration through
  PrivateLink for MongoDB 6.0+ sharded Atlas clusters.

* Decomissions [free monitoring](manual/administration/free-monitoring).

- Introduces the following billing improvements:

  - Lowers RPU billing for serverless instances with read-heavy
    workloads.

  - Supports viewing and analyzing Atlas usage with a billing cost
    explorer. To learn more, see [Billing Cost Explorer](atlas-billing).

  - Supports viewing resource tags in Atlas billing invoices through
    the Atlas Administration API and billing invoice CSV (Comma-Separated Values) exports. To learn
    more, see [Resource Tags on Invoices](resource-tags-on-invoices).

- Provides preview of using Terraform Provider v1.11.1 or higher to
  manage your workforce's access to MongoDB Atlas with OIDC
  (OpenID Connect). To learn more, see
  [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization).

* Supports [Online Archives](online-archive-overview) as a source
  for Atlas Data Federation. To learn more, see
  [Online Archives](adf-configuration-file-oa).

* Adds a new [project overview](project-overview) that displays
  modules containing common Atlas actions. You can configure the
  project overview to display as your project's landing page.

- Provides preview of MongoDB 7.0+ databases support for dedicated clusters.

- Supports cross-organization billing in [Atlas for Government](atlas/government).

- Releases v2.0 of the [Atlas Admin API](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec).
  To learn more, see [versioning](api-versioning-overview).

  > **Important:** This version unifies the API (Application Programming Interface) endpoints for single-cloud clusters
  > and [Configure High Availability and Workload Isolation](create-cluster-multi-region) clusters
  > under the [Clusters](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-clusters) API (Application Programming Interface) resource.

* Supports [Tags on Clusters](database-deployment-tags) for your Atlas
  clusters through the Atlas UI, Atlas Administration API,
  and Atlas CLI.

* Provides preview of [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization) to MongoDB 7.0+ databases on
  Atlas using an identity provider (IdP) that supports OpenID
  Connect (OIDC), including Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, and Ping Identity.

* Introduces resource-level [versioning](api-versioning-overview)
  in the Atlas Administration API.

* Provides [Go SDK](atlas-admin-sdk) to fetch or modify data from
  the Atlas Administration API.

* Introduces new [Organization Billing Viewer](<Organization Billing Viewer>) [Atlas User Roles](user-roles) to restrict billing access to authorized users.

* Supports an optional `federationSettingsId` parameter in the
  [createOneOrganization](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-clusters) API (Application Programming Interface)
  endpoint to link an Atlas organization to an existing
  [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization)
  configuration.

* Introduces fine-grained data modification and scalability improvements
  for time series data.

- Supports one new Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Microsoft Azure](microsoft-azure):

  - `qatarcentral` (Doha, Qatar)

- Supports five new Google Cloud [Google Cloud Platform (GCP)](google-gcp):

  - `me-west1` (Tel Aviv, Israel)

  - `europe-west12` (Turin, Italy)

  - `me-central1` (Doha, Qatar)

  - `us-east5` (Columbus, OH, USA)

  - `us-south1` (Dallas, TX, USA)

- Sets the limit of unique [Shard a Global Collection](shard-global-collection)
  per Atlas [project](projects) to 40.
  To learn more, see [Atlas Limits](atlas-limitations).

* Supports five new AWS (Amazon Web Services) [Amazon Web Services (AWS)](amazon-aws):

  - `ap-south-2` (Hyderabad, India)

  - `ap-southeast-4` (Melbourne, Victoria, Australia)

  - `eu-central-2` (Zurich, Switzerland)

  - `eu-south-2` (Spain)

  - `me-central-1` (UAE)

* Adds the ability to pull a source sharded cluster to an Atlas
  sharded cluster for source and destination clusters running
  MongoDB 6.0.5 or later. To learn more, see [Live Migrate a MongoDB 6.0.5 or Later Cluster into Atlas](c2c-pull-live-migration).

* Upgrades each of your clusters that run MongoDB 4.2 to MongoDB 4.4.
  MongoDB 4.2 reached end of life on 30 April 2023, according to the
  [MongoDB Support Policy](https://www.mongodb.com/support-policy?tck=docs). The upgrade to
  MongoDB 4.4 runs within your maintenance window if you
  [maintenance](atlas-modify-project-settings).

- Updates the default MongoDB version for all new Atlas
  clusters to MongoDB 6.0.

  - Automatically upgrades all free tier
    (`M0`) and shared tier (`M2` and `M5`) clusters to
    MongoDB 6.0.

- Supports enabling a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy) to
  protect your backup data.

- Improves the [Require IP Access List for the Atlas UI](atlas-ui-ip-access-list) for the
  Atlas UI so that you can use the same IP access list to restrict
  API access to Atlas, and access to the Atlas UI.

- Supports user-defined metrics labels from Atlas to [Datadog](datadog-integration).

* Supports the ability to live migrate the source clusters to Atlas
  destination clusters for replica sets running MongoDB 6.0.5 or later.
  To learn more, see [Live Migrate a MongoDB 6.0.5 or Later Cluster into Atlas](c2c-pull-live-migration).

- Introduces generally available Atlas integrations for AWS (Amazon Web Services)
  CloudFormation and the AWS (Amazon Web Services) Cloud Development Kit (CDK). To learn
  more, see [MongoDB Atlas Integrations for AWS CloudFormation and CDK are now Generally Available](https://www.mongodb.com/blog/post/atlas-integrations-aws-cloud-formation-cdk-now-generally-available).

* Adds SCRAM-SHA-256 as the default authentication mechanism for database
  users in Atlas. To learn more, see [Configure Database Users](mongodb-users).

* Supports simulating an outage for Atlas for regions that contain a
  majority of database nodes, and reconfiguring a cluster from an
  unhealthy to a healthy state in the event of such an outage.

* Supports connecting to your database behind private endpoints with an
  optimized SRV connection string for sharded clusters.

* Adds a streamlined experience for users deploying their first Atlas
  database using templates for best practices.

* Adds EU region support for the PagerDuty integration.

Supports converting Shared clusters (`M0`, `M2`, `M5`) to Serverless instances.
